title: leucanthemum vulgare var. crassifolium
intro: Leucanthemum vulgare var. crassifolium, commonly known as the field daisy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Leucanthemum vulgare var. crassifolium are typically made by infusing the flowers in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties.
